monday.label: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling
monday.description: Local arts scene, festivals, creative economy, media, and cultural tech
monday.theme_description: Today we explore how Cariboo and rural BC communities create, preserve, and share culture — from festivals and community radio to digital archives and open-source arts tools. We're looking for stories where creativity meets technology in small-town contexts, and where local artists and storytellers are finding new ways to reach audiences beyond geography.
monday.scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: digital storytelling platforms and tools, community media tech (podcasting, streaming, local publishing), creative tech economy in small towns, rural cultural sector innovation, open-source arts tools, Cariboo arts festivals using digital outreach, preservation of rural cultural heritage through technology, animation/film/media production in rural BC, local radio/podcast infrastructure, and how rural communities use technology for creative expression. Prefer articles that bridge culture AND technology in rural/Cariboo contexts. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

tuesday.label: Working Lands & Industry
tuesday.description: Cariboo forestry, ranching, mining, agriculture — tech in the resource economy
tuesday.theme_description: Today's focus is the resource economy that defines the Cariboo — forestry, ranching, mining, and the agricultural technology helping these industries adapt and modernize. We're looking for stories about the people and innovations keeping rural BC's working landscape viable, including tensions between resource extraction and long-term stewardship, and the technology bridging both.
tuesday.scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Working Lands & Industry' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: precision forestry and remote sensing in Cariboo logging, AgTech and ranch management systems, mining automation and safety technology, farm sensors and soil monitoring, sawmill modernization and automation, drone surveys for resource management, IoT in agriculture and ranching, climate adaptation tech for working lands, workforce training for industrial tech, commodity tracking and supply chain tech, and how Cariboo resource industries adopt innovation. Prefer articles that bridge resource economy AND technology in rural/industrial contexts. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

wednesday.label: Community Tech & Governance
wednesday.description: SD27, CRD, Williams Lake council, municipal networks, civic innovation
wednesday.theme_description: Today we look at how Cariboo communities govern themselves and use technology for public good — from school district digital initiatives and rural broadband projects to council decisions and civic innovation. We're interested in stories where technology and local democracy intersect, and how small municipalities are solving big problems with limited resources.
wednesday.scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Community Tech & Governance' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: municipal broadband and fiber projects in Cariboo communities, SD27 education technology and digital learning, civic tech platforms and open data initiatives, community-owned networks and ISPs, smart rural infrastructure (water, waste, transit), digital government services for rural areas, council decisions on technology spending, public Wi-Fi and connectivity programs, election technology and civic engagement platforms, rural innovation hubs, and how Williams Lake/Quesnel/100 Mile/CRD use technology for public services. Prefer articles that bridge governance AND technology in rural civic contexts. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

thursday.label: Indigenous Lands & Innovation
thursday.description: Tŝilhqot'in, Secwépemc, Dakelh — language, governance, land stewardship, and tech
thursday.theme_description: Today we explore how Cariboo and BC Indigenous nations are using technology on their own terms — for language revitalization, land stewardship, cultural preservation, and self-determined governance. We're looking for stories that centre Indigenous voices and leadership in innovation, where technology serves community sovereignty rather than replacing it.
thursday.scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Indigenous Lands & Innovation' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: language revitalization technology for Tŝilhqot'in/Secwépemc/Dakelh, Indigenous guardian programs using remote monitoring and sensors, land management tech and environmental monitoring on Indigenous lands, digital tools for traditional knowledge preservation, community mapping and GIS for land claims, Indigenous-led connectivity projects, technology for cultural heritage documentation, mobile apps for language learning, drone/satellite monitoring for stewardship, reconciliation through digital collaboration, and how Cariboo Indigenous nations use innovation for self-determination. Prefer articles that bridge Indigenous governance/culture AND technology. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

friday.label: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life
friday.description: Chilcotin wilderness, backcountry recreation, conservation tech, wildfire and climate
friday.theme_description: Today we head into the wild — the Chilcotin plateau, river valleys, and backcountry that define the Cariboo experience. We're covering the technology helping monitor, protect, and navigate these landscapes: wildfire detection, wildlife tracking, satellite communication, and the tools keeping backcountry travellers safe and conservationists informed.
friday.scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life' through a RURAL TECH AND INNOVATION lens. Strong fits include: wildfire detection tech and BC Wildfire monitoring systems, satellite/drone surveillance for Chilcotin wilderness, wildlife tracking and conservation technology, watershed sensors and salmon habitat monitoring, weather stations and climate tracking in backcountry, avalanche forecasting tech, trail mapping and GPS systems, outdoor recreation apps and connectivity, backcountry communication devices (satellite messengers, Meshtastic), flood/drought prediction systems, remote cameras for wildlife research, and how technology helps manage and protect Cariboo wild spaces. Prefer articles that bridge wilderness/conservation AND technology. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.

saturday.label: Cariboo Voices & Local News
saturday.description: Williams Lake, Quesnel, 100 Mile House, Bella Coola — community stories and digital equity
saturday.theme_description: Today is all about the Cariboo-Chilcotin: the decisions, events, and conversations happening in Williams Lake, Quesnel, 100 Mile House, Bella Coola, and the surrounding region. We're covering local government, community life, economic stories, and the digital equity challenges that shape what it means to live and work in rural BC.
saturday.scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Cariboo Voices & Local News' — stories FROM and ABOUT the Cariboo-Chilcotin region and its communities. Score 80-100 for articles directly covering: news and events in Williams Lake, Quesnel, 100 Mile House, Bella Coola, Horsefly, Likely, Anahim Lake, Alexis Creek, or the Cariboo Regional District; local government and civic decisions (City of Williams Lake, City of Quesnel, CRD, District of 100 Mile House); local health services and emergency closures; local economy, businesses, and community programs; Cariboo arts, culture, and community events; AND digital equity, rural internet access (fiber, fixed wireless, Starlink), and connectivity serving these specific communities. Articles from local Cariboo news sources (mycariboonow.com, wltribune.com, quesnelobserver.com, 100milefreepress.net, cfjctoday.com) covering Cariboo community life should score 65-85. Score 50-79 for: BC provincial news directly affecting Cariboo communities, rural Canadian community stories closely paralleling Cariboo life, digital equity and rural internet broadly. Score 20-49 for: general BC news that may affect rural areas, broader rural Canada topics without specific Cariboo tie-in. Score 0-19 for: national/international news without Cariboo relevance, pure tech or entertainment with no community angle. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match.

sunday.label: Science, Wonder & the Natural World
sunday.description: Scientific discovery, natural phenomena, ecology, and the curiosity behind how our world works — grounded in the landscapes and living systems of the Cariboo
sunday.theme_description: Today we look at how Cariboo and rural communities are building lasting resilience — adapting to economic transitions, climate pressures, and the challenges of isolation. From off-grid energy and community broadband to post-resource-economy diversification and cooperative models, these are the stories about how rural BC is not just surviving change but shaping its own future.
sunday.scoring_prompt: Rate how well this article fits the theme 'Resilient Rural Futures' — stories about how Cariboo and rural communities are adapting, diversifying, and building lasting strength. Strong fits include: post-resource-economy transition and community economic planning (post-mill, post-mine, post-forestry towns finding new paths), local economic diversification initiatives and community futures discussions, rural workforce transition and skills development, co-operative and community-ownership economic models, off-grid power systems and solar for Cariboo homes, mesh networks and community-owned connectivity, self-hosted systems and data sovereignty, telehealth platforms and remote healthcare tech, climate adaptation technology for rural areas, emergency communication systems and disaster tech, renewable energy innovation, food security tech (greenhouses, vertical farms, local food tracking), water monitoring and conservation systems, affordable housing innovation, resilient infrastructure design, rural remote work tools and digital nomad infrastructure, and future-focused solutions that help Cariboo communities thrive despite isolation or climate challenges. Score equally highly for community economic resilience stories and technology resilience stories — both are core to rural futures. Score 0-100 where 100 is a perfect thematic match and 0 is completely unrelated.
